Politics.headline: Ultra-Orthodox conscription dispute pushes Israeli government to brink
Politics.detail: A dispute over mandatory military service for ultra-Orthodox Jewish men is threatening to collapse the Israeli government.

Politics.headline: Trump waffles on Taiwan arms deal after Xi talks
Politics.detail: President Trump said he's unsure whether he would greenlight a planned $14 billion weapons package for Taiwan after discussing arms sales with Chinese President Xi Jinping, signaling a potential shift in US policy towards Taiwan.

Politics.headline: Supreme Court rejects bid to restore Virginia congressional map favoring Democrats
Politics.detail: The Supreme Court turned away Virginia Democrats seeking to reinstate a new election map that would have favored Democrats in 10 of its 11 congressional districts.

Politics.headline: Trump-Xi summit: China, US disagree on what they agreed on
Politics.detail: Despite a push to extend a truce, the two leaders did not resolve differences on key issues like Iran and Taiwan, even though they moved towards a business-first relationship.

Politics.headline: Border Patrol chief Mike Banks resigns in latest Trump immigration shakeup
Politics.detail: U.S. Border Patrol chief Mike Banks resigns, marking the latest in a series of high-profile departures from the Department of Homeland Security under the Trump administration.

Finance & Economy.headline: India's April exports jump 13.8%, trade gap widens to 3-month high
Finance & Economy.detail: India's exports saw a significant increase in April, but the trade deficit also widened, reaching a 3-month high, indicating a complex economic situation.

Finance & Economy.headline: Petrol, diesel price hike may trigger chain reaction across economy, say experts
Finance & Economy.detail: Experts warn that the increase in petrol and diesel prices could have a cascading effect throughout the economy, potentially leading to broader inflationary pressures.

Finance & Economy.headline: Iran war pushed inflation to highest rate in nearly three years
Finance & Economy.detail: The ongoing war in Iran has caused a surge in gasoline prices, contributing to an overall increase in inflation, which has now reached its highest level in nearly three years.

Finance & Economy.headline: US Import Prices Rise More than Expected
Finance & Economy.detail: US import prices increased more than anticipated, primarily driven by a sharp rise in fuel and lubricant costs, marking the largest monthly increase since March 2022.

Finance & Economy.headline: Pound heads for worst week in 18 months as Burnham lines up Labour bid
Finance & Economy.detail: The British pound is experiencing its worst week in the past 18 months amidst political developments, as Burnham prepares to contend for Labour leadership.

Technology.headline: Govt. plans AI training centres at upazila level: Faqir Mahbub
Technology.detail: Posts, Telecommunications and Information Technology Minister Faqir Mahbub Anam announced plans to establish AI and freelancing training centers at every upazila level in Bangladesh, alongside expanding 5G services and high-speed internet connectivity across the country, with the goal of training the young generation in AI and freelancing so that they can develop skills and earn substantial income abroad. The government's next priority would be ensuring reliable and high-speed internet for all, while reducing the digital divide between urban and rural areas through expansion of last-mile fibre connectivity nationwide.

Technology.headline: Meta Introduces Writing Gestures and Walking Directions to Ray-Ban Smart Glasses
Technology.detail: Meta is enhancing its augmented reality smart glasses by adding writing gestures and walking directions, offering developers the option to create their own services for the platform. This update aims to make the smart glasses more useful and interactive for users.

Technology.headline: Microsoft adds more former Ai2 researchers, bolstering its Superintelligence team
Technology.detail: Microsoft's Superintelligence team has been strengthened with the addition of at least 10 former Allen Institute for AI staffers and researchers, including the core of the institute's OLMo open-source model effort. These departures from Ai2 occur amidst a shift in the institute's funding towards applied uses of AI.

Technology.headline: AI-Powered Military Systems Raise International Concerns
Technology.detail: Global defense experts are debating the risks of autonomous AI-powered military systems, as several nations have revealed advanced drone and surveillance technologies, raising ethical and security challenges. Governments are now discussing global AI warfare standards during diplomatic meetings due to intensifying technological competition.

Technology.headline: Distributors trade tips on artificial intelligence during Denver event
Technology.detail: At a recent event in Denver, distributors discussed the use of AI and automation in the industry, which includes predictive AI for machinery maintenance and wearable technology for worker safety. Attendees noted that while AI implementation is still in early stages, companies investing in AI are seeing net growth in the economy.

Science.headline: Scientists Reversed Memory Loss by Recharging the Brain's Tiny Engines
Science.detail: Researchers have shown that malfunctioning mitochondria, the cell's energy generators, may directly cause cognitive decline in neurodegenerative diseases, and scientists restored memory performance in mouse models of dementia by temporarily boosting mitochondrial activity in the brain. This hints that energy failure inside neurons could happen before brain cells die, potentially offering a new target for future Alzheimer's treatments.

Science.headline: Scientists Find Hidden Brain Nutrient Deficit That May Fuel Anxiety
Science.detail: A major analysis of brain scans found that people with anxiety disorders have noticeably lower levels of choline, a nutrient crucial for healthy brain function. The strongest evidence appeared in the prefrontal cortex, the region tied to emotional control and decision-making, suggesting new nutrition-based treatments could be developed.

Science.headline: Scientists discover hidden “brakes” that stop massive earthquakes
Science.detail: Researchers have discovered natural underwater “brakes” that keep a Pacific fault from unleashing even bigger earthquakes. The fault contains hidden “brake zones” where seawater and unusual rock structures work together to stop quakes from becoming even larger, potentially improving earthquake models.

Science.headline: First-ever direct image of the cosmic web reveals the Universe's hidden highways
Science.detail: Astronomers have revealed the sharpest image ever captured of a filament in the cosmic web, the enormous hidden structure connecting galaxies across the Universe. The glowing strand stretches 3 million light-years and links two galaxies from nearly 12 billion years ago, providing new insight into how galaxies are fueled and formed.

Science.headline: PCOS has a new name, Neanderthals were the world's oldest dentists, and the first nuclear bomb explosion spawned an 'alien' crystal
Science.detail: Polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS) is officially renamed to polyendocrine metabolic ovarian syndrome (PMOS) to better reflect the nature of the disease, and a 60,000-year-old Neanderthal molar reveals evidence of early dentistry practices. An 'extreme' crystal formed in the 1945 nuclear bomb test is unlike anything scientists have seen.

Health.headline: Ebola outbreak in Congo kills dozens, Africa's CDC says
Health.detail: A new Ebola outbreak in a remote province in Congo has caused 65 deaths and 246 suspected cases so far, Africa CDC reports.

Health.headline: FDA Approves ctDNA-Guided Atezolizumab for Bladder Cancer
Health.detail: The FDA has approved ctDNA-guided Atezolizumab for bladder cancer. This approval marks a step forward in personalized cancer treatment based on circulating tumor DNA analysis.

Health.headline: 'Research here is world class': son of Steve Jobs looks to invest in UK cancer care.
Health.detail: Reed Jobs, son of Steve Jobs, is looking to invest in UK cancer care, noting the world-class research being conducted in the UK.

Health.headline: New Ebola outbreak in Congo kills dozens as Uganda confirms separate case
Health.detail: A new Ebola outbreak in Congo has resulted in dozens of deaths, while Uganda has confirmed a separate case. Health officials are battling the new epidemic.

Health.headline: Is Cannabis Safe After 65? Stanford Experts Reveal 5 Risks Older Adults Should Know
Health.detail: Stanford experts warn that cannabis use among older adults is rising rapidly, and today's marijuana is much more potent, leading to underestimated risks.

Sports.headline: Yanks' Schlittler shuts down Mets in Subway Series; Holmes fractures right leg
Sports.detail: After going 8-4 against four sub-.500 teams, the Mets again were victimized by their own offense as Schlittler dominates. Holmes suffered a leg fracture during the game.

Sports.headline: Scheffler remains in hunt at halfway despite 'absurd' pin positions at US PGA
Sports.detail: Alex Smalley and Maverick McNealy lead on four under as the world's best golfers struggled in the US PGA Championship at Aronimink.

Sports.headline: Tentative $2.3B Deal For New Rays Ballpark In Tampa Announced
Sports.detail: A tentative deal has been reached for a new Rays Ballpark in Tampa, with a cost of $2.3 billion.

Sports.headline: Detroit PWHL adds Rheaume as GM
Sports.detail: The Professional Women's Hockey League (PWHL) team in Detroit has appointed Rheaume as their General Manager.

Sports.headline: NFL insists Vrabel-Russini scandal not considered in scheduling Patriots for 2026 kickoff game
Sports.detail: The NFL insists that the alleged scandal involving Vrabel and Russini was not a factor in the decision to schedule the Patriots for the 2026 kickoff game.

Entertainment.headline: Drake Shocks Internet With Triple Album Release
Entertainment.detail: Drake just dropped three new albums in one day, titled 'Iceman,' 'Habibti,' and 'Maid of Honour'. Fans are rushing to listen to the unexpected releases.

Entertainment.headline: Taylor Swift Steps Out in Bridal White as Travis Kelce Wedding Approaches!
Entertainment.detail: With the wedding of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce drawing near, Taylor was seen wearing white, exciting fans and fueling wedding speculation.

Entertainment.headline: Sunil Grover becomes a superstar, roasts Bollywood with hilarious act.
Entertainment.detail: Comedian Sunil Grover's act roasting Bollywood at the Chetak Screen Awards 2026 has gone viral, with many praising his wit and humor.

Entertainment.headline: John Travolta surprised with honorary Palme d'Or at Cannes Film Festival
Entertainment.detail: The Cannes Film Festival surprised John Travolta with an honorary Palme d'Or, celebrating his contributions to cinema.

Entertainment.headline: Clarence Carter, US soul star who had hits with Patches and more, dies aged 90.
Entertainment.detail: Clarence Carter, the acclaimed US soul singer known for hits like 'Patches,' has passed away at the age of 90, leaving behind a rich musical legacy.

Crime & Justice.headline: Iraqi National Arrested and Charged with Providing Material Support to Iranian-Backed Terrorist Organizations and Directing Attacks Targeting U.S. Citizens and Interests
Crime & Justice.detail: An Iraqi national has been arrested and charged with providing material support to Iranian-backed terrorist organizations and directing attacks targeting U.S. citizens and interests. The suspect is accused of plotting terror attacks in the U.S., including targeting a Jewish institution in New York.

Crime & Justice.headline: Michigan Home Health Care Agency Owner Convicted of $1.6M Medicare Fraud Scheme and Kickback Conspiracy
Crime & Justice.detail: The owner of a Michigan home health care agency has been convicted of a $1.6 million Medicare fraud scheme and kickback conspiracy. Brett Blackman was convicted on charges including healthcare and Medicare fraud and faces decades in prison.

Crime & Justice.headline: Maryland Tax Return Preparer Sentenced to Prison for Preparing False Returns
Crime & Justice.detail: A Maryland tax return preparer has been sentenced to prison for preparing false tax returns. The preparer was found guilty of fraud.

Crime & Justice.headline: Fraud Division Announces Massive Crackdown for Second Straight Week — Over $1 BILLION in Nationwide Fraud Enforcement Actions
Crime & Justice.detail: The Justice Department's Fraud Division announced a massive crackdown for the second straight week, involving over $1 billion in nationwide fraud enforcement actions. This highlights the ongoing efforts to combat fraud across the country.

Crime & Justice.headline: Broughton teacher faces 19 charges for child sex crimes over 10-year span.
Crime & Justice.detail: A Broughton teacher has been charged with 19 child sex crimes over a 10-year span and is now banned from Scouting America. The teacher faces multiple charges related to child sex offenses.

Education.headline: Adults locked out of learning to access education with new reform
Education.detail: The UK government is enacting reforms to help adults access education opportunities.

Education.headline: National Leaders of Governance for further education
Education.detail: Guidance has been released regarding National Leaders of Governance for further education.

Education.headline: Early years initial teacher training (ITT) providers
Education.detail: New guidance has been published for early years initial teacher training (ITT) providers.

Education.headline: Halifax school librarian, social worker positions up in the air after changes to funding.
Education.detail: Changes to funding are putting Halifax school librarian and social worker positions at risk, as councillors recently voted to ratify a new five-year supplementary education funding agreement that prioritizes music and fine arts.

Education.headline: NEET-UG paper leak row: Exam to go fully computer-based from next year, says Education Minister Pradhan
Education.detail: In response to the NEET-UG paper leak controversy, Education Minister Pradhan announced that the exam will be fully computer-based starting next year.

Lifestyle.headline: Influencer Exposes Cannes Reality: 'Paid Rs 44,500 For 7 Videos, 7 Photos'
Lifestyle.detail: An influencer revealed the costs associated with creating content at the Cannes Film Festival, highlighting the business side of social media influencing.

Lifestyle.headline: Dua Lipa stuns in a metallic dress and serpentine necklace at Cannes 2026
Lifestyle.detail: Dua Lipa made a striking appearance at Cannes 2026, wearing a metallic dress and a serpentine necklace.

Lifestyle.headline: Swimming pools, fabulous views and radical architecture: 30 UK holiday cottages with the wow factor
Lifestyle.detail: A selection of UK holiday cottages are highlighted for their impressive swimming pools, stunning views, and unique architectural designs.

Lifestyle.headline: Alex Hassell: 'A wasp flew in my ear in front of Jude Law – he couldn't see the wasp so just saw me freak out'
Lifestyle.detail: Alex Hassell recounts an amusing anecdote about an incident with a wasp during a scene with Jude Law.

Lifestyle.headline: PCOS is now called PMOS. What the name change means for care.
Lifestyle.detail: The condition formerly known as PCOS is now referred to as PMOS, and this change may impact patient care and understanding of the condition.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Supporting frontline healthcare
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial discusses amendments to the Medical Care Act in Taiwan that mandate nurse-to-patient ratios, highlighting the need for broader reforms to address structural issues in the healthcare system and the concerns of nurses.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Opinion: Colorado's public media is building something stronger together
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Colorado's public media outlets are merging to reduce duplication, operate more efficiently, and reinvest resources into journalism, music programming, and community engagement, while maintaining editorial autonomy and distinct local brands.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Daily Editorial Analysis 16 May 2026
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The fusion of economics and geopolitics has fundamentally reshaped the international order, with trade, technology and supply chains becoming central to global competition, positioning India strategically due to its scale and stability.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: Do Wecht, Fetterman, Catena point to changing Democratic party?
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The article discusses how some Democrats in Pennsylvania, like Fetterman and Catena, are frustrating members of their own party with their political realignments and stances on certain issues, reflecting a diverse spectrum within the Democratic party.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL — Protective custody
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Senators are reportedly employing a concept of "protective custody" to shield Sen. Ronald dela Rosa from apprehension by state forces.

World.headline: 8 Dead, 20 Injured After Cargo Train Rams Bus In Bangkok
World.detail: A cargo train struck a bus in Bangkok at a railway crossing where vehicles had stopped, resulting in multiple fatalities and injuries.

World.headline: At least 80 deaths reported as Congo grapples with new Ebola outbreak
World.detail: A new Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo has resulted in at least 80 deaths, posing a significant challenge to the region.

World.headline: Iran warns of more economic consequences for US over its 'war of choice'
World.detail: Iran has issued a warning to the United States about potential economic repercussions due to what it refers to as the US's 'war of choice'.

World.headline: Israel's military says Hamas commander killed in airstrike on Gaza
World.detail: The Israeli military has announced the killing of a Hamas commander in an airstrike targeting Gaza.

World.headline: 1996 Military Jet Killings: Why Team Trump Wants Cuba's Raul Castro Charged
World.detail: Team Trump is pursuing charges against Cuba's Raul Castro regarding the 1996 incident where Cuban jets shot down planes flown by the Brothers to the Rescue group.

Zimbabwe.headline: 'Zimbabwe is well positioned to source adequate fuel amid Middle East tensions', National Oil Company of Zimbabwe CEO tells Parly
Zimbabwe.detail: The National Oil Company of Zimbabwe CEO told Parliament that Zimbabwe is in a good position to source enough fuel despite tensions in the Middle East.

Zimbabwe.headline: Local Government ministry flags illegal land sales in Chinhoyi
Zimbabwe.detail: The Ministry of Local Government has reported illegal land sales occurring in Chinhoyi.

Zimbabwe.headline: Retired Chief Justice Malaba defends judiciary, tells accusers to bring evidence of political meddling and corruption
Zimbabwe.detail: Retired Chief Justice Malaba defended the judiciary, challenging those accusing it of political interference and corruption to provide evidence.

Zimbabwe.headline: Mutare man jailed for cyber bullying businessman
Zimbabwe.detail: A man from Mutare has been sentenced to jail for cyber bullying a local businessman.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zim industrialisation agenda needs diaspora backing
Zimbabwe.detail: The Zimbabwe industrialization agenda requires support from the diaspora community.
